---
title: "gender_slur_s3_da_osf"
output: html_document
date: "2025-07-03"
---

```{r}
data <- read_csv("data_s3_clean.csv")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# Calculate summary statistics for target_gender x pejorative combinations
summary_stats <- data %>%
  group_by(target_gender, pejorative) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_rating = mean(selected_option, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_rating = sd(selected_option, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_rating = median(selected_option, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_rating = sd_rating / sqrt(n),
    ci_lower = mean_rating - 1.96 * se_rating,
    ci_upper = mean_rating + 1.96 * se_rating,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print("Summary Statistics by Target Gender and Pejorative Type:")
print(summary_stats)

# Wide format table
summary_wide <- summary_stats %>%
  select(target_gender, pejorative, mean_rating) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = pejorative, values_from = mean_rating)

print("Mean Ratings in Wide Format:")
print(summary_wide)

# Visualization: Target Gender x Pejorative interaction
ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = target_gender, y = mean_rating, 
                         color = pejorative, group = pejorative)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),
    width = 0.1,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.03)
  ) +
  geom_line(size = 0.8, position = position_dodge(width = 0.03)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.03)) +
  labs(
    x = "Target Gender",
    y = "Acceptability",
    color = "Pejorative Type"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("female_target" = "Female Target", 
                             "male_target" = "Male Target")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(3, 6), breaks = seq(3, 6, by = 0.5)) +
  # Colorblind-friendly palette (Paul Tol's palette)
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "COI" = "#117733",    # Green
    "GOI" = "#CC6677"     # Rose/Purple
  )) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  )
```
```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)  # For p-values

# Ensure factors are properly coded
data$target_gender <- as.factor(data$target_gender)
data$pejorative <- as.factor(data$pejorative)
data$submission_id <- as.factor(data$submission_id)
data$itemname <- as.factor(data$itemname)

# Add random slopes for both factors by items
model_complex1 <- lmer(selected_option ~ target_gender * pejorative + 
                      (1 + target_gender + pejorative | submission_id) + 
                      (1 + target_gender + pejorative | itemname), 
                      data = data)
summary(model_complex1)

```


```{r}
# Calculate summary statistics for individual tokens by target gender AND pejorative
token_stats <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(token)) %>%
  group_by(target_gender, pejorative, token) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_rating = mean(selected_option, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_rating = sd(selected_option, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_rating = sd_rating / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print("Summary Statistics by Target Gender, Pejorative, and Token:")
print(token_stats)

# Improved visualization (fixed)
ggplot(token_stats, aes(x = reorder(token, mean_rating), y = mean_rating)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_rating, 2)), 
            hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_grid(pejorative ~ target_gender, 
             scales = "free",
             labeller = labeller(
               target_gender = c("female_target" = "Female Target", 
                                "male_target" = "Male Target"),
               pejorative = c("COI" = "COI Terms", "GOI" = "GOI Terms")
             )) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Naturalness Ratings by Token, Target Gender, and Pejorative Type",
    x = "Token",
    y = "Mean Rating (1-7)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", color = "white"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.margin = margin(1, 1.5, 1, 1, "cm"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "darkgray")  # Removed alpha argument
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02)))
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(cluster)

# Calculate means, SDs, and sample sizes for CI calculation
token_stats <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(token)) %>%
  group_by(token, pejorative, target_gender) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rating = mean(selected_option, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_rating = sd(selected_option, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Calculate gender differences with 95% CIs
token_analysis <- token_stats %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = target_gender, 
    values_from = c(mean_rating, sd_rating, n),
    names_sep = "_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    gender_diff = mean_rating_female_target - mean_rating_male_target,
    # Standard error of the difference
    se_diff = sqrt(sd_rating_female_target^2/n_female_target + sd_rating_male_target^2/n_male_target),
    # Degrees of freedom (conservative approach)
    df = pmin(n_female_target - 1, n_male_target - 1),
    # 95% CI bounds
    ci_lower = gender_diff - qt(0.975, df) * se_diff,
    ci_upper = gender_diff + qt(0.975, df) * se_diff
  )

# Continue with clustering
silhouette_scores <- c()
for(k in 2:4) {
  km <- kmeans(token_analysis$gender_diff, centers = k, nstart = 25)
  sil <- silhouette(km$cluster, dist(token_analysis$gender_diff))
  silhouette_scores[k-1] <- mean(sil[, "sil_width"])
}

optimal_k <- which.max(silhouette_scores) + 1
set.seed(123)
final_clustering <- kmeans(token_analysis$gender_diff, centers = optimal_k, nstart = 25)
token_analysis$cluster <- final_clustering$cluster

# Add bias type labels
token_analysis$bias_type <- case_when(
  token_analysis$cluster == which.max(final_clustering$centers) ~ "Female",
  token_analysis$cluster == which.min(final_clustering$centers) ~ "Male",
  TRUE ~ "Neutral"
)

# Display results with CIs - FIXED: include cluster in select
print("Clustering results with 95% CIs:")
print(token_analysis %>% 
      select(token, pejorative, gender_diff, ci_lower, ci_upper, cluster, bias_type) %>% 
      mutate(
        gender_diff = round(gender_diff, 3),
        ci_lower = round(ci_lower, 3),
        ci_upper = round(ci_upper, 3)
      ) %>%
      arrange(cluster, desc(gender_diff)))

# Variance explained
total_variance <- var(token_analysis$gender_diff) * (nrow(token_analysis) - 1)
within_cluster_variance <- final_clustering$tot.withinss
variance_explained <- (total_variance - within_cluster_variance) / total_variance
print(paste("Variance explained by clustering:", round(variance_explained * 100, 1), "%"))
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(cluster)
token_analysis <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(token)) %>%
  group_by(token, pejorative, target_gender) %>%
  summarise(mean_rating = mean(selected_option, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = target_gender, values_from = mean_rating, names_prefix = "rating_") %>%
  mutate(gender_diff = rating_female_target - rating_male_target)
silhouette_scores <- c()
for(k in 2:4) {
  km <- kmeans(token_analysis$gender_diff, centers = k, nstart = 25)
  sil <- silhouette(km$cluster, dist(token_analysis$gender_diff))
  silhouette_scores[k-1] <- mean(sil[, "sil_width"])
}
optimal_k <- which.max(silhouette_scores) + 1
print(paste("Optimal number of clusters:", optimal_k))
print("Silhouette scores:")
print(data.frame(k = 2:4, silhouette = round(silhouette_scores, 3)))
set.seed(123)
final_clustering <- kmeans(token_analysis$gender_diff, centers = optimal_k, nstart = 25)
token_analysis$cluster <- final_clustering$cluster

# ADD THIS: Create bias_type labels
token_analysis$bias_type <- case_when(
  token_analysis$cluster == which.max(final_clustering$centers) ~ "Female-biased",
  token_analysis$cluster == which.min(final_clustering$centers) ~ "Male-biased",
  TRUE ~ "Neutral"
)

print("Clustering results:")
print(token_analysis %>% 
      select(token, pejorative, gender_diff, cluster, bias_type) %>% 
      arrange(cluster, desc(gender_diff)))
# ADD THIS: Variance explained (the key robustness measure)
total_variance <- var(token_analysis$gender_diff) * (nrow(token_analysis) - 1)
within_cluster_variance <- final_clustering$tot.withinss
variance_explained <- (total_variance - within_cluster_variance) / total_variance
print(paste("Variance explained by clustering:", round(variance_explained * 100, 1), "%"))
```

```{r}
# Clean minimal bar chart with colorblind-friendly colors
ggplot(token_analysis, aes(x = reorder(token, gender_diff), y = gender_diff, 
                          fill = bias_type)) +
  # Add reference line
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Bars
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Flip coordinates for better readability
  coord_flip() +
  
  # Clean labels
  labs(
    x = "Insult",
    y = "Gender Difference Score",
    fill = "Bias Type"
  ) +
  
  # Colorblind-friendly palette (Paul Tol's palette)
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Female-biased" = "#CC6677",  # Rose
    "Male-biased" = "#117733",    # Green  
    "Neutral" = "#DDCC77"         # Sand
  )) +
  
  # Clean y-axis
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-2, 1, 0.5)) +
  
  # Clean professional theme
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  )
```
